metadata.title: China's AI stack beyond DeepSeek — the full domestic LLM field, the Huawei Ascend hardware push, and the built-in state-alignment / censorship layer
metadata.status: labs, models, chip specs, market shares, and the CAC regulatory facts are FACT (web-verified); the 'capability is real AND censorship is built-in' dual reading is the graded finding; no intent imputed beyond the documented regulation
metadata.frame: The corpus's efficiency counter-thesis previously named only DeepSeek; in reality China fields a DEEP, competitive, largely OPEN-WEIGHT LLM ecosystem AND a mandatory state-alignment layer baked into all of it. Three layers: (1) the LABS — Alibaba (Qwen), DeepSeek, Zhipu (GLM), Moonshot (Kimi), MiniMax, StepFun, 01.AI, Baichuan, Baidu (Ernie), Tencent (Hunyuan), ByteDance (Doubao), Xiaomi (MiMo), iFlytek; (2) the HARDWARE push to escape US export controls — Huawei Ascend (910C/950), Cambricon, Biren, Moore Threads, fabbed by SMIC; (3) the CENSORSHIP layer — the Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC) requires algorithm registration + 'Core Socialist Values' testing, so every public model self-censors on Xi/Tiananmen/Taiwan by design. The twist: many models are OPEN-WEIGHT (MIT/Apache on Hugging Face), so the censorship can be stripped downstream — the bridge to the local/uncensored-AI counter-ecosystem. Ties to fin-google-amazon-anthropic-meta (the efficiency/Jevons thesis), spec-semiconductor-logistics-standards (SMIC/export controls), macro-crqc (China programs), and the planned uncensored-AI block.

the_labs: Far beyond DeepSeek. Alibaba's Qwen (Qwen3.5, Apache 2.0) is the most-used open-weight family globally; DeepSeek (High-Flyer; V3.2/R-series) remains the efficiency standard-bearer; the 'AI Tigers' startups are Zhipu AI (GLM-4.6/GLM-5 — notably trained ENTIRELY on Huawei Ascend), Moonshot AI (Kimi K2.x — agentic-benchmark leader, up to 100 parallel sub-agents), MiniMax (M2.x — matches frontier models at ~1/20th the cost, MIT-licensed), StepFun, 01.AI (Yi, Kai-Fu Lee), and Baichuan. Incumbents: Baidu (Ernie/Wenxin), Tencent (Hunyuan), ByteDance (Doubao), Xiaomi (MiMo — which by Q2-2026 reportedly led OpenRouter at ~21% weekly-token share, ~3x OpenAI), and iFlytek (Spark). Market signal: by April 2026 the combined share of Xiaomi+Alibaba+MiniMax+Zhipu+DeepSeek+StepFun exceeded ~45% of weekly token volume on OpenRouter. grade: fact (labs, models, licenses, shares).
the_hardware_push: To escape US export controls, China is building a sovereign accelerator stack. Huawei Ascend is the spearhead: the 910C (96GB HBM2e, ~A100-class FP16, built on SMIC 7nm) targets ~600,000 units in 2026 (up to ~1.6M dies incl. other models), with the Ascend 950PR (Q1 2026) and 950DT (Q4 2026) + SuperPoD interconnect following. Cambricon, Biren, and Moore Threads (GPUs) round out the field; SMIC fabs them (the 7nm-via-DUV ceiling). REALITY CHECK: the substitution is incomplete — DeepSeek's R2 was reportedly DELAYED by training instabilities on Ascend, forcing a return to (export-allowed, older) Nvidia H800s for critical runs; HBM remains the bottleneck. So the hardware-independence claim is real-and-improving but NOT yet achieved. grade: fact (specs, volumes, the R2/Ascend setback).
the_censorship_layer: Every public-facing generative-AI service in China must (a) register its algorithm with the Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC) and pass a security assessment — 748 services filed nationally by Dec 2025 — and (b) uphold 'Core Socialist Values': models must not generate content that could 'subvert state power, harm national security, or undermine social stability'. CAC actively TESTS models (ByteDance, Alibaba, et al.) on responses about Xi Jinping and politically sensitive topics; models refuse or deflect on Tiananmen 1989, Taiwan, Xinjiang, Hong Kong by design. This is alignment-by-state-mandate — a structurally different control regime from Western RLHF safety tuning (which is corporate/voluntary). grade: fact (the Interim Measures 2023, the registration count, the socialist-values testing).
the_open_weight_paradox: The censorship is baked into the WEIGHTS and the serving layer — but most of these models ship OPEN-WEIGHT (Qwen Apache 2.0; GLM/MiniMax MIT; many on Hugging Face). Open weights can be fine-tuned/'abliterated' to remove refusals downstream (the de-censoring tooling — Heretic and similar — and uncensored re-releases). So China's models simultaneously (a) carry mandated censorship when served from China, and (b) become de-censorable raw material once the weights are public — the paradox that bridges this block to the local/uncensored-AI counter-ecosystem. grade: fact (open licenses) + the de-censorability is demonstrated (abliteration) but quality-variable (contested).
the_honest_reading: Both true at once: (1) Chinese AI CAPABILITY is real, plural, fast-moving, and unusually open — not one DeepSeek but a dozen competitive labs, several at frontier-adjacent quality and far lower cost, increasingly on domestic silicon; (2) the CENSORSHIP is real, mandatory, and built-in — a state-alignment layer Western models don't have. The efficiency/Jevons counter-thesis holds (cheap, abundant, open models accelerate diffusion), but it must be stated with the control caveat (the abundance is state-aligned at the source). And the open-weight paradox means the control is leaky once weights escape. The composition guard applies: 'China's models' is shorthand for many independent labs under a common regulator, not one mind. Overlay; excluded from the proofs.

edges.raw_instrument: Qwen (Qwen3.5, Apache 2.0) — most-used open-weight family globally
GLM-4.6/GLM-5 (AI Tiger; trained entirely on Huawei Ascend)
M2.x — frontier-adjacent at ~1/20th cost, MIT (AI Tiger)
algorithm registration + 'Core Socialist Values' testing (748 services filed by Dec 2025)
mandatory built-in alignment — models refuse on Xi/Tiananmen/Taiwan/Xinjiang by design
Cyberspace Administration of China — the state content regulator
Ministry of Industry and IT — industrial AI/chip policy
Ascend 910C (96GB HBM2e, SMIC 7nm) -> 950PR/950DT; ~600k units 2026; built to escape US Entity-List / AI-chip export controls
fabs the Ascend/domestic accelerators (7nm via DUV ceiling)
domestic compute substrate (GLM-5 trained entirely on Ascend) — substitution incomplete
reverted to (export-allowed) Nvidia H800s for critical training after Ascend instabilities (R2 delay)
